info.name: OmniCharge - User Service
info.description:
## OmniCharge - User Service

This collection contains the **User Service API** for the **OmniCharge** platform. It covers the complete set of CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations for managing users within the system.

**Base URL:** `http://localhost:8081`

---

### Overview

The User Service is responsible for handling all user-related operations on the OmniCharge platform — from registering new users to retrieving, updating, and removing existing ones. All requests in this collection are organized under the **User Management APIs** folder.

---

### Key Capabilities

| Operation | Method | Endpoint | Description |
|-----------|--------|----------|-------------|
| Register User | `POST` | `/users/register` | Create and register a new user in the system |
| Get All Users | `GET` | `/users` | Retrieve a list of all registered users |
| Get User By ID | `GET` | `/users/{id}` | Fetch details of a specific user by their ID |
| Update User | `PUT` | `/users/{id}` | Update the profile information of an existing user |
| Delete User | `DELETE` | `/users/delete/{id}` | Remove a user from the system by their ID |

---

### Getting Started

1. Ensure the User Service is running locally on port `8081`.
2. Use the **Register User** request to create a new user account.
3. Use **Get All Users** or **Get User By ID** to retrieve user data.
4. Use **Update User** to modify an existing user's details.
5. Use **Delete User** to remove a user from the system.

request.description:
## Register User

Registers a new user in the system by accepting their personal details. On successful registration, the endpoint returns the newly created user's basic information.

---

### Endpoint Details

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Method** | `POST` |
| **URL** | `http://localhost:8081/users/register` |
| **Content-Type** | `application/json` |

---

### Request Body Parameters

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | Full name of the user |
| `email` | `string` | Email address of the user |
| `password` | `string` | Password for the account (must be strong — include uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters) |
| `contactNo` | `string` | Contact number of the user |

#### Sample Request Body

```json
{
  "name": "Aditya Chaudhary",
  "email": "aditya@gmail.com",
  "password": "Aditya@1234",
  "contactNo": "9876553210"
}
```

---

### Response

#### Success — `200 OK`

On successful registration, the API returns the registered user's `name`, `email`, and `contactNo`.

```json
{
  "name": "Aditya Chaudhary",
  "email": "aditya@gmail.com",
  "contactNo": "9876553210"
}
```

## Get All Users

Retrieves a list of all registered users in the system.

---

### Request Details

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Method   | `GET` |
| URL      | `http://localhost:8081/users` |

### Request Body

No request body required.

---

### Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

Returns a JSON array of user objects.

#### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Full name of the user |
| `email` | string | Email address of the user |
| `contactNo` | string | Contact number of the user |

#### Example Response

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Aditya Chaudhary",
    "email": "adi@gmail.com",
    "contactNo": "9876543210"
  }
]
```

## Get User By ID

Retrieves the details of a specific user by their unique ID. Returns user profile information including name, email, and contact number.

---

### Endpoint

**Method:** `GET`
**URL:** `http://localhost:8081/users/:userId`

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type   | Required | Description                        |
|-----------|--------|----------|------------------------------------|
| `userId`  | number | Yes      | The unique identifier of the user. |

**Example:** `/users/2` — fetches the user with ID `2`.

---

### Sample Response

**Status:** `200 OK`

```json
{
  "name": "Aditya Chaudhary",
  "email": "adi@gmail.com",
  "contactNo": "9876543210"
}
```

---

### Response Fields

| Field       | Type   | Description                              |
|-------------|--------|------------------------------------------|
| `name`      | string | Full name of the user.                   |
| `email`     | string | Email address associated with the user.  |
| `contactNo` | string | Contact phone number of the user.        |

---

### Notes

- If the user with the given `userId` does not exist, the server may return a `404 Not Found` response.
- The `contactNo` field is returned as a string to preserve leading zeros and formatting.

## Update User

Updates the details of an existing user identified by their unique user ID. This endpoint accepts a JSON body with the fields to be updated and returns the updated user object on success.

---

**Method:** `PUT`  
**URL:** `http://localhost:8081/users/{id}`

> **Note:** The user ID is a **path parameter** embedded in the URL (e.g., `/users/2`). Make sure to replace `{id}` with the actual numeric ID of the user you want to update. 
  

---

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as `raw JSON` (`application/json`).

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | String | The updated full name of the user. |
| `email` | String | The updated email address of the user. |
| `password` | String | The updated password for the user account. |
| `contactNo` | String | The updated contact/phone number of the user. |

#### Sample Request Body

``` json
{
  "name": "Aditya Updated",
  "email": "adi@gmail.com",
  "password": "Adi@12321",
  "contactNo": "9999958399"
}

 ```

---

### Response

#### `200 OK` — User updated successfully

Returns the updated user object. Note that the `password` field is **not** returned in the response.

``` json
{
  "name": "Aditya Updated",
  "email": "adi@gmail.com",
  "contactNo": "9999958399"
}

 ```

---

### Notes

- The `id` in the URL path must correspond to a valid, existing user.
    
- The `password` field is accepted in the request but is intentionally excluded from the response for security reasons.
    
- All fields in the request body are expected to be provided; partial updates may not be supported depending on the server implementation.

## Delete User

Deletes an existing user from the system by their unique ID. Once deleted, the user record is permanently removed.

---

### Method & URL

```
DELETE http://localhost:8081/users/delete/:id
```

---

### Path Parameter

| Parameter | Type   | Required | Description                          | Example |
|-----------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------|---------|
| `id`      | number | Yes      | The unique ID of the user to delete. | `3`     |

**Example URL:** `http://localhost:8081/users/delete/3`

---

### Response

**200 OK** — Returns a plain text confirmation message upon successful deletion.

```
User with Id: 3 has been deleted!
```

---

### Saved Example

A saved example named **"Delete User"** is available under this request. It demonstrates a successful `200 OK` response with the plain text confirmation message returned after deleting the user with ID `3`.

response.name: Delete User
response.body: User with Id: 3 has been deleted!
